QF.id: VII Market Participants in Securities Markets_2

question.text: Which of the following entities falls under Category I Foreign Portfolio Investor (FPI)?

answer.text: Sovereign wealth funds and central banks

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Category I FPIs include: (i) Government and Government-related investors like central banks, sovereign wealth funds, multilateral organizations; (ii) entities 75%+ owned by government entities; (iii) pension funds and university funds; (iv) appropriately regulated entities (banks, AMCs, insurance); (v) FATF member country entities meeting conditions; (vi) entities with Cat I investment managers or 75%+ owned by eligible entities. **The Trap:** Family offices, corporate bodies, and charitable organizations fall under Category II FPI, not Category I. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series I and VIII cover FPI participation in derivative markets. IFSCA-01 covers FPI registration for IFSC entities. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Category I = government-related + regulated entities. Category II = everyone else (corporates, family offices, charities, individuals, unregulated funds). Category II FPIs who are corporates/individuals/family offices are treated as NIIs.
